title: Strong illposedness of incompressible Euler in borderline spaces
speaker: Dong Li

proof_stages.label: Critical Sobolev barrier from Euler energy estimates
proof_stages.summary: The Euler equations and standard local well-posedness theory are set up, and the energy estimate shows that closing the critical norm requires controlling ||u||_infty, while the pressure/Riesz-transform term blocks naive endpoint or two-norm refinements.

proof_stages.label: Endpoint theory and negative evidence
proof_stages.summary: The known Hölder, Sobolev, and Besov well-posedness results are reviewed, showing that critical results require the Besov l^1 summability loophole, while Takada's divergence-free commutator counterexamples, a two-dimensional vorticity computation, and earlier partial pathologies motivate endpoint ill-posedness.

proof_stages.label: Strong ill-posedness theorem and formulation
proof_stages.summary: The folklore conjecture is sharpened into dense strong norm inflation: arbitrarily small or arbitrarily nearby critical data generate solutions whose critical norm is infinite on every positive time interval, with precise two-dimensional and three-dimensional critical-space statements.

proof_stages.label: Large Lagrangian deformation
proof_stages.summary: For two-dimensional Euler in vorticity form, the proof first chooses small vorticity data whose flow map has a very large, essentially hyperbolic derivative while keeping the low norms controlled.

proof_stages.label: Conversion of deformation into critical norm inflation
proof_stages.summary: A localized high-frequency perturbation is inserted so that the critical H^1 vorticity norm detects the large derivative of the flow map, with a higher-regularity perturbative argument controlling the induced change of the flow.

proof_stages.label: Infinite patch iteration and gluing
proof_stages.summary: The local inflation mechanism is repeated on infinitely many separated or carefully nested patches, using transport speed and kernel decay in the noncompact case and a multiscale interaction analysis in the compact case to produce infinite norm inflation.

proof_stages.label: Three-dimensional adaptation and stretching control
proof_stages.summary: The strategy is adapted to three dimensions by confronting vorticity stretching, nonlocal critical norms, lifespan issues, and the metric factors in the axisymmetric no-swirl reduction through additional perturbative and L^infty controls.

proof_stages.label: Multiscale Lagrangian-Eulerian synthesis
proof_stages.summary: The proof is summarized as a robust multiscale construction combining Lagrangian deformation with Eulerian norm detection, and its ill-posed critical behavior is contrasted with critical well-posedness in dispersive models.

examples.note: The comparison H^1=B^1_{2,2} in two dimensions versus B^1_{2,1} shows concretely how replacing dyadic l^2 summation by l^1 recovers the L^infty embedding at critical regularity, motivating the Besov endpoint theory.
